name: Twine (Python Package Upload Tool)

auth.notes: PyPI API token via TWINE_USERNAME/__token__ + TWINE_PASSWORD; Trusted Publishing (OIDC) preferred

performance.notes: Upload speed depends on package size and PyPI throughput

agent_readiness.notes: Standard Python package upload tool. twine upload dist/*. twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*. twine check dist/* (validates metadata before upload). TWINE_USERNAME=__token__ TWINE_PASSWORD=pypi-xxx. Trusted Publishing via GitHub Actions OIDC (no token needed). twine check catches common metadata errors before upload. Works with any build backend (setuptools, flit, hatch, poetry). The final step in Python package release workflow.

gotchas: Cannot overwrite existing PyPI versions (delete not allowed on PyPI)
API token required (username/password deprecated for PyPI)
twine check only validates basic metadata, not full PyPI requirements
Trusted Publishing (OIDC) preferred for CI — avoids token management
best_when: Uploading Python packages to PyPI after building with any build tool
Validating distribution metadata before upload with twine check
avoid_when: When poetry publish or flit publish handles the full workflow
When GitHub Actions Trusted Publishing is configured (OIDC is better)
summary: Twine is the standard PyPI upload tool: twine upload dist/* after building with any backend. twine check dist/* validates metadata. Supports TestPyPI, API tokens, and GitHub Actions Trusted Publishing (OIDC — preferred over tokens). The de facto standard final step in Python package release pipelines. Works with setuptools, flit, hatch, or poetry-built distributions.
